title: pedilonum flammula
intro: Pedilonum flammula, also known as flame flower or flame of the woods, is a plant species in the family Pedilanthaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Pedilonum flammula include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
